method_26_h.summary: The Dotfuscator decryption stub. Fully disassembled.
method_26_h.algorithm_confirmed: ToCharArray; key = 0x6DDC67B5 + salt; for each char: lo = (char & 0xFF) XOR (key&0xFF); key++; hi = ((char>>8)&0xFF) XOR (key&0xFF); key++; swap lo/hi; recompose char as (hi<<8) | lo; Intern result string.

method_1088_k_method_1090_i_method_1092_.differences: Method[1088] uses XOR+ADD, Method[1090] uses ADD+ADD, Method[1092] uses AND+ADD on limbs (per-limb operations differ).
method_1088_k_method_1090_i_method_1092_.common_pattern: Iterate two ushort-array limbs; for each i: tmp = a[i] OP b[i]; tmp += carry; carry = (tmp & T_0972) >> ((carry & 0x3F)); a[i] = tmp
method_1088_k_method_1090_i_method_1092_.interpretation: Schoolbook multi-precision integer arithmetic: 1088=XOR-and-carry, 1090=ADD-and-carry, 1092=AND-and-carry. The XOR variant is unusual — could be a custom hash-mix step.

method_1098_j.summary: 43-byte bignum trim/right-shift helper. Walks down from len-1 looking for first non-zero limb, then resizes via Method[1102]:d.

method_1101_h_BIGNUM_MULTIPLY.key_ops: ldsfld T0x4000971; mul (multiply by the static field constant)

method_1103_g_BIGNUM_DIVMOD.key_ops: div; Math.Floor; rem; conv.i4 conversion + per-limb shl/shr with mask 0x3F (6-bit) and bigger 32-bit limb math via T_0972
method_1103_g_BIGNUM_DIVMOD.interpretation: Implements schoolbook long-division on multi-limb integers. Mask 0x3F = 63 indicates 64-bit-word ops (limb is uint32 with shift count limited to 0-63).

method_1104_b.summary: 45-byte buffer-allocation helper. Computes ceil(arg1 / T_0971) + 1 limbs.
method_1104_b.use: Allocates the result buffer for a bignum operation.

method_1107_d_STRING_TO_BIGNUM.summary: 203-byte string -> bignum decoder. Walks string chars, looks up via static T_0979 table, multiplies-add via T_0974, calls Method[26]:h to decrypt portions inline.
method_1107_d_STRING_TO_BIGNUM.use: Parses W7 catalog's per-ECU (n, o, p) ASCII parameters into BigInteger form.

method_1301_l.summary: 337-byte HEX STRING TO BYTE ARRAY converter.
method_1301_l.key_ops: Sets up 16-element character lookup [0-9A-F] = [48..57, 65..70]; parses 2 hex chars at a time via MR[505]:ToByte; outputs byte array.
method_1301_l.use: Converts the W7's intermediate hex output back to raw bytes for the final cipher result.

static_fields_used_as_cipher_constants.T0x04000971: Used in shl/shr/mul/rem operations as the limb size constant.
static_fields_used_as_cipher_constants.T0x04000972: Used as bitmask for limb-truncate operations.

summary: The W7 cipher is a CUSTOM multi-precision integer algorithm using ushort-limb math. The 6 first-layer methods (1075/1079/1080/1087/1089/1091/1096/1106/1126) wrap the 9 second-layer primitives (1088/1090/1092/1097/1098/1101/1103/1104/1107). Method[1101]:h is bignum MULTIPLY, Method[1103]:g is bignum DIV/MOD. Limb size is uint16 (ldelem.u2) with shift masks of 0x3F = 6 bits (= 64-bit constants). The cipher fits the W7 PROVENANCE entry: 'big-integer cipher with 360 per-ECU (n, o, p) parameter triples'. Translating to JS requires reading 2 more static field values (T_0971, T_0972) from the FieldRVA section.
